title: Add visible EN/FR language toggle button to navigation bar

requirements.text: Add a clearly visible EN/FR toggle button to the top navigation bar in `frontend/src/components/Header.tsx`. The button must appear in both desktop and mobile nav menus. Clicking it should toggle between English (EN) and French (FR) language states. The button should be visually distinct — styled with a clear label showing the current language and a separator (e.g., 'EN | FR') with the active language highlighted — using the existing TMU AI DAO color tokens from `frontend/src/index.css`. Implement a language context or state (e.g., a React context or local state lifted to the Header/Layout level) so the selected language can be consumed by page components. Do NOT change any other UI elements, routes, or backend logic.

quotes: The EN/FR language toggle button is not appearing in the navigation menu.
Please add a visible EN/FR toggle button in the top navigation bar that users can click to switch between English and French.

acceptanceCriteria: An EN/FR toggle button is visible in the top navigation bar on desktop viewports.
An EN/FR toggle button is visible in the mobile navigation menu on small viewports.
The active language is visually highlighted (e.g., bold, different color, or underline) compared to the inactive language.
Clicking the button toggles the language state between 'EN' and 'FR'.
The button renders without errors and does not break existing navigation links or layout.
No other UI components, routes, or backend code are modified.

constraints: Do not modify any backend files.
Do not change any existing navigation links, layout, or page components beyond wiring up the language context.
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Use existing TMU AI DAO color tokens and Tailwind utility classes for button styling.
nonGoals: Translating page content to French (only the toggle button UI is in scope for this request).
Persisting language preference to the backend or localStorage.
Adding any new routes or pages.
Changing any other visual design or layout elements.
